bird.name: Moss-backed tanager
bird.summary: The moss-backed tanager (Bangsia edwardsi) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is found in Colombia and Ecuador.

locations.name: 哥伦比亚
locations.summary: 哥伦比亚共和国（西班牙語：República de Colombia），通稱哥倫比亞（西班牙語：Colombia），是一個位於南美洲西北部的国家，同時是拉丁美洲第四大國家，為南美洲國家聯盟的前成員國（2018年8月退出）。它的北部是加勒比…

locations.name: 厄瓜多尔
locations.summary: 厄瓜多尔共和国（西班牙語：República del Ecuador），通稱厄瓜多尔（Ecuador），是位於南美洲西北部的國家，北面與哥倫比亞相鄰，南接祕魯，西面為太平洋，另包括距離厄瓜多本土1,000公里的加拉巴哥群島。 厄瓜多於18…

locations.name: 安地斯山脈
locations.summary: 安地斯山脈（西班牙語：Cordillera de los Andes；奇楚亞語：Walla Antikuna）是地球陸地上最長的山脈，位於南美洲的西岸，約7,000公里長 (或8,900公里长)，200至700公里寬（最寬的部份在南緯18…

relations.subject: Moss-backed tanager
relations.evidence: It is endemic to the Western Andes of western Colombia and northwestern Ecuador, where it inhabits dense, humid Pacific slope forests

relations.subject: Moss-backed tanager
relations.evidence: Its population is currently thought to be decreasing, mainly due to habitat loss caused by continuing deforestation, gold mining, and fumigation to combat narcotics cultivation
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

relations.subject: Moss-backed tanager
relations.evidence: Its population is currently thought to be decreasing, mainly due to habitat loss caused by continuing deforestation, gold mining, and fumigation to combat narcotics cultivation
relations.object_summary: 原生林被砍伐导致栖息地面积和连通性下降。
